meta_title: For Founders: Antigravity project template to Solve no project ideas (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to Antigravity project template and overcome no project ideas. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Founders: Antigravity Project Template for AI SaaS
content.seo_description: Overcome the blank page syndrome. Learn to use the Antigravity project template and Claude Code to launch high-converting AI SaaS MVPs in record time.
content.answer_block: The Antigravity template solves the 'no project ideas' trap by providing a high-velocity boilerplate that forces rapid prototyping. By integrating it with Claude Code, you shift from ideation to production-ready architecture in minutes, allowing you to validate market demand through functional shipping rather than endless conceptualizing.
content.intro:
The graveyard of SaaS startups is littered with brilliant ideas that never saw a single line of production code. You are likely suffering from analysis paralysis—the suffocating belief that you need the 'perfect' idea before you start building. Stop waiting for inspiration; start building the infrastructure that attracts it.

True founders don't wait for the muse; they build the machine. The Antigravity project template acts as your architectural skeleton, stripping away the friction of boilerplate setup so you can focus on the only thing that matters: shipping value to users who pay you.

steps.title: Initialize the Antigravity Skeleton
steps.description: Establish the foundational project structure to minimize configuration debt.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Initialize a new SaaS project using the Antigravity starter template with authentication, database ORM, and Stripe integration pre-configured.

steps.title: Define the Business Logic Layer
steps.description: Translate your problem statement into a functional database schema that supports monetization.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a schema for a subscription-based AI utility that tracks user credits and usage history.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Create Prisma schema for User, Subscription, and UsageLog models'

steps.title: Implement the Core AI Agent
steps.description: Integrate the LLM layer to transform the boilerplate into a value-generating engine.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Integrate Claude 3.5 Sonnet to process user inputs according to the core value proposition defined in the system prompt.

steps.title: Build the Monetization Hook
steps.description: Ensure your MVP has a clear path to revenue by enabling Stripe Checkout.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure the Stripe webhook handler to update subscription status in the database upon successful payment.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit 'Implement Stripe webhook controller for plan upgrades'

steps.title: Deploy to Production
steps.description: Ship your project to a live environment to start gathering real-world feedback.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Prepare the deployment configuration for a production-ready Vercel build.

pro_tips: Always favor 'boring' tech stacks; your goal is to validate the business, not to experiment with bleeding-edge frameworks.
Use Claude Code to write your integration tests first; this documents your expected behavior and speeds up debugging.
Keep your initial feature set to exactly one 'killer' function; anything more is a distraction from your primary value prop.
Implement analytics immediately; if you can't measure user behavior, you're just guessing, not building.
Treat your code as disposable assets; be prepared to rewrite the entire MVP once you find your first 10 paying customers.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, focus entirely on cold outreach and user feedback. Iterate based on actual usage data, not your internal assumptions.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in a vacuum and start shipping products that solve real problems. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
